name: San Luis Green Chile Soup
recipeIngredient: 1/2 lb Boned chicken breast, thinly sliced
1/2 lb Pork butt steak, thinly sliced
1/2 cup Peeled, seeded and diced tomatoes
recipeInstructions:
1. Roast Anaheim chilies, red bell pepper, and jalapeno under hot broiler close to heat until skins blister and turn all sides charred. Place in plastic bag for 10 minutes. Peel, dice, and set aside.
2. In a large skillet, cook salt pork until fat is rendered. Remove with slotted spoon and drain on paper towel. Increase heat and sauté chicken and pork quickly until browned. Drain and set aside.
3. Melt butter in a large saucepan. Add onion and cook until transparent. Add flour and cook stirring until roux is golden brown. Remove roux from heat and stir in chili powder, cumin, garlic, and tomato sauce. Whisk in warm chicken broth and heat to simmering.
4. Add chilies, red pepper, jalapeno, salt pork, chicken, and pork to soup. Heat thoroughly and stir in tomatoes and cilantro. Simmer until heated through.
